items.title: Micron stock jumps over 16% in premarket trading after blockbuster earnings
items.summary: The company's revenue more than quadrupled from $9.3 billion a year earlier to $41.46 billion, it reported on Wednesday
items.label: earnings

items.title: SK Hynix surges 12% after Micron earnings; blockbuster Nasdaq listing
items.summary: Shares of South Korean chip giant SK Hynix surged 12% on Thursday after the company said it is seeking to raise as much as $29.4 billion.
items.label: earnings

items.title: Micron is tech's new margin king as memory crisis pushes company past Nvidia and Meta
items.summary: Micron said in its earnings report that its gross margin jumped to 84.9% from 39% a year ago.
items.label: earnings

items.title: Cerebras CEO says margin forecast was 'misunderstood' as stock plummets after earnings
items.summary: AI chipmaker Cerebras has a staggered lock-up expiration that includes some shares becoming available for trading this week.
items.label: earnings

items.title: JPMorgan Chase unveils $50 billion buyback, Goldman Sachs raises dividend after Fed stress test
items.summary: The announcements followed the release of the Federal Reserve's annual stress test, which found that all 32 large banks weathered a hypothetical recession.
items.label: capital

items.title: Chevron CFO says gas prices will normalize after Trump presses Big Oil
items.summary: Her comments come shortly after President Donald Trump ordered an investigation into Big Oil, accusing them of "gouging" consumers.
items.label: management

items.title: South Korea’s IPO bust clouds equity markets as Chaebol structure restrains listings
items.summary: South Korea's IPO activity has lagged that of its regional peers as the structure of the market and governance reforms collide.
items.label: capital

items.title: Trump meets defense CEOs as Iran operations strain U.S. missile stockpiles
items.summary: The White House is pressing major defense firms to expand missile and munitions output as Iran talks and weapons stockpile concerns put new pressure on the Pentagon’s industrial base.
items.label: management

items.title: These hidden PCE inflation triggers could force a Fed rate hike — and shock the market
items.summary: A hot PCE report will ignite Wall Street’s fear of a restrictive Fed.
items.label: other

items.summary: The Fed's annual exercise comes at a pivotal moment for bank regulation because, unlike previous years, the results will not affect capital requirements.
items.label: other

items.title: Federal student loans have a new interest rate discount — here's who qualifies
items.summary: To qualify for the U.S. Department of Education's new interest rate discount on student loans, borrowers must meet several requirements. Here's what to know.
items.label: other

items.title: Gold hovers around $4,000, silver holds below $60 — has the shimmer worn off the precious metal rally?
items.summary: Gold is down almost 8% so far this year, while silver has shed more than 20%.
items.label: market

items.title: Wall Street is coalescing on a single S&P 500 target. The power of round numbers is irresistible.
items.summary: Wall Street, and Main Street, love their round numbers, as at least four strategists have the same 8,000 year-end target on the S&P 500.
items.label: market

items.title: Iran warns ships it's 'unacceptable and dangerous' to transit the Strait of Hormuz without their approval
items.summary: The stern warning underscores Tehran's resolve to retain control over the Strait of Hormuz and to resist transits that bypass its authorization.
items.label: product

items.title: Energy secretary says U.S. has ended Iran's ability to close Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Some 72 ships loaded with 19 million barrels of oil have passed through Hormuz in the past 24 hours, Energy Secretary Chris Wright said.
items.label: product

items.title: Retail investors have suddenly got more cautious in using options and margins. That may spell trouble for tech, says JPMorgan.
items.summary: JPMorgan’s research suggests retail investors are becoming more cautious about levrerage after the market’s recent rally and this may spell trouble for the tech sector in which they have mostly focused.
items.label: other

items.title: Amazon adds new funding, lifting India AI and cloud investment to $48 billion
items.summary: Amazon announces fresh investment of $13 billion to expand AI and cloud infrastructure in India, taking its total investment to $48 billion.
items.label: other

items.title: My son passed away. Am I crazy to move to my former home and sell a house I bought in September?
items.summary: “I raised my children there as a single mother, and it was the last home my son lived in before he passed away unexpectedly.”

items.summary: Rheinmetall and other European defense stocks fell for a second day, after Germany scrapped the F126 naval program, raising procurement concerns.

items.title: Inside India newsletter: Meet the humans teaching robots to perform routine tasks, as India finds a way to enter the AI race
items.summary: Several companies have cropped up in India providing video training data made by humans that is being used to teach robots in the U.S. and China.

items.summary: White House Office of Management and Budget Director Russell Vought asked House Speaker Mike Johnson for billions in extra spending for the Iran war.

items.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of campaign to 'brazenly' and 'illicitly' extract AI capabilities
items.summary: The letter, which was obtained by CNBC, claims Alibaba carried out "the largest known distillation attack on Anthropic to date."

items.title: Identity theft victims face 'unconscionable' IRS delays, report says
items.summary: Victims currently face wait times of almost two years to resolve their cases, according to the National Taxpayer Advocate.

items.title: Qualcomm stock pops 15% after chipmaker almost doubles projection for 2029 non-handset revenue
items.summary: Qualcomm's primary business has been smartphones, which accounted for two-thirds of the company's product revenue in it's most recent quarter.

items.title: Trump cancels signing of bipartisan housing bill ahead of tense meeting with GOP senators
items.summary: President Donald Trump abruptly canceled the signing of a bill aimed at increasing housing affordability an hour before he was due at the Capitol for the ceremony.

items.title: Toyota gains on General Motors in new U.S. sales forecast: 'GM may be looking over their shoulder'
items.summary: Toyota has notably leaned into hybrid vehicles while GM and others bet on all-electric vehicles, which saw lower-than-expected adoption from consumers.
